focus: correctness

findingDetails.title: Recycle stress re-models the production replay path instead of driving it
findingDetails.evidence: The stress helper reimplements production replaySnapshot and the load assertions use that helper; production replaySnapshot enqueues socket/output frames and is module-private, while existing integration tests do not cover successful-recycle unsafe pending mutation error emission under load.
findingDetails.claim: The new recycle stress proves replayable reads survive and unsafe in-flight mutations get -32001 under load.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched launcher-session-proxy.vitest.ts for createSessionProxy integration coverage of unsafe pending requests; found classification, reattach-exhaustion, and single replay/protocol-drift coverage, but no successful reattach unsafe pending mutation output assertion.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The parallel model may be intentional because replaySnapshot is module-private and the stress avoids sockets.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade/close if a production-path test drives successful reattach with replayable and unsafe pending requests and asserts fresh-socket replay plus exactly one -32001 client error for unsafe requests.

findingDetails.title: Checkpoint stress assertion masks a missing snapshotFormat return field
findingDetails.evidence: The test uses created.snapshotFormat ?? 'v2', so it passes when snapshotFormat is absent even though createCheckpointV2 currently returns snapshotFormat: 'v2'.

findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Rows may have been authored before the later serverInfo bump, but they now contradict the reviewed target.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if these rows are explicitly declared immutable historical snapshots superseded by a newer canonical traceability surface.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update 002/003 specs, plans/tasks, and implementation summaries that cite context-server.ts:1014 to 1.8.0 or move pre-fix values into historical context.

findingDetails.title: Tool-count traceability contradicts canonical TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length

findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: package.json description may be old marketing metadata, but it remains in review scope and user-visible.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the broader count is explicitly separated from mk-spec-memory and package metadata is declared non-authoritative.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile package metadata and feature_catalog root counts to 36 or clearly separate any cross-server count from TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length.

findingDetails.title: Durability README claims checkpoint catalog stays bounded after v2 restores
findingDetails.claim: The durability README says checkpoint-v2 contention keeps the catalog bounded at MAX_CHECKPOINTS.
findingDetails.evidence: The stress test explicitly says catalog rows may outnumber on-disk dirs because v2 restore merges older catalog rows back in, and it asserts only live on-disk dirs <= MAX_CHECKPOINTS. The child spec and decision record use the on-disk invariant, and restoreCheckpointV2 merges live checkpoint catalog rows after the file swap.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked create-time pruning, restore catalog merge, listCheckpoints, child spec, decision record, implementation summary, and stress assertions for any source that intentionally bounds restored catalog rows.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The README sentence may have meant create-time pruning rather than post-restore catalog count, but it is attached to a create+restore stress row and conflicts with the test's explicit post-restore comment.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if maintainers define catalog in this README row as shorthand for on-disk snapshot directory catalog, or if a source-backed note clarifies create-time-only pruning while leaving restore catalog merge behavior intact.
findingDetails.recommendation: Change the README row to say the on-disk snapshot directory set stays bounded by MAX_CHECKPOINTS and no orphaned snapshot/tmp dirs survive; do not claim the checkpoint catalog row count is bounded after v2 restore.

findingDetails.title: Feature catalog labels the proxy replayable set as read-mostly even though memory_save is replayable
findingDetails.evidence: The root catalog summarizes REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES as read-mostly, but launcher-session-proxy.cjs includes memory_save in REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES and classifies any name in that set as replayable. Unsafe destructive tools remain separately excluded, so impact is documentation precision rather than safety.
findingDetails.claim: Root feature catalog says the proxy replays safe in-flight read-mostly requests named by REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the per-feature front-proxy entry, the actual replayable set, classifyFrame, and the unsafe set; unsafe destructive tools remain excluded and the per-feature entry uses non-exhaustive example wording.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: memory_save may be intentionally replay-safe through dedup/idempotency, but then the root catalog should describe the set as replayable/idempotent rather than read-mostly.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Remove or downgrade if memory_save is removed from REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES or if the root catalog wording is explicitly scoped as non-exhaustive examples rather than a label for the set.

findingDetails.title: EX-039 overstates when move reconciliation will fire
findingDetails.claim: EX-039 is executable as a packet-identity move validation: moving a tracked spec doc to a new path under the same packet identity should produce moveReconciled > 0 and update the row in place without re-embedding.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked source and tests for a broader old-vs-new packet-id comparison. Source uses only the new path packet_id and matches old paths by same basename plus same grandparent; the test covers only old-name/spec.md to new-name/spec.md sibling-folder rename.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The scenario may intend same packet identity to mean sibling spec-folder rename with the same basename, but that precondition is not stated in the command or expected result.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if a separate source proves broad old/new packet-id reconciliation, or if the playbook is constrained to the actual sibling-folder-rename preconditions and sandbox/revert workflow.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact searches and direct reads covered the EX-039 playbook entry, master-index entry, scan call site, move-reconcile implementation, and move-reconcile unit test; no prior registry finding covered this EX-039 scenario-specific overstatement.

focus: traceability - README cluster: serverInfo 1.8.0, TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length, SPECKIT_BACKEND_ONLY, error codes, schema v28-v30

findingDetails.title: README proxy replay boundary is mislabeled as read-only
findingDetails.claim: The README cluster tells operators that the front-proxy transparently replays read-only tools across a recycle.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the two README surfaces in the assigned cluster and the proxy allow-list/classifier; no README caveat names memory_save or says the replay boundary includes non-read mutating calls.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: memory_save may be intended to be safe or idempotent enough for replay, and the source excludes more destructive tools via UNSAFE_TOOL_NAMES. That still does not make it read-only, and the source comment calls secondary-index idempotency a known follow-up.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if maintainers define memory_save replay as read-only-equivalent in operator docs, or if source removes memory_save from REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES so the README boundary becomes literally true.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search plus direct reads found read-only replay wording in both README surfaces and non-read replayable memory_save in shared proxy source.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace read-only with replayable/idempotent wording and explicitly mention memory_save, or remove memory_save from REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES if the intended contract is truly read-only replay.

focus: correctness - stress durability assertion strength and contract accuracy

findingDetails.title: Enrichment stress overstates backfill coverage for deferred marker rows
findingDetails.evidence: The stress claims every marker and the partial-index-eligible incomplete set drain to complete, and its helper counts all rows where post_insert_enrichment_status != 'complete'. Production status includes deferred; repairEnrichmentOnReplay leaves deferred untouched; repairIncompleteMarkers selects only pending, partial, and failed; existing unit coverage asserts deferred rows are not repaired by replay or backfill. The stress only proves pending flood convergence, not full partial-index drain semantics.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads of enrichment-state.ts and enrichment-state.vitest.ts show this is limited to stress assertion/prose coverage; production deferred behavior already has focused unit coverage.
findingDetails.recommendation: Narrow the stress prose/helper naming to pending/partial/failed repairable backlog or add a dedicated assertion that deferred rows remain deferred and are excluded from bounded backfill.

graph_status.notes: Graph readiness stale; used graphless direct reads and exact searches.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Existing R1-P2-001 and R5-P1-001 cover the remaining observed checkpoint stress/catolog assertion weaknesses.

searchLedger.rationale: No new non-duplicate daemon replay finding found; prior active finding covers the model-vs-production gap.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Deduped against R1-P1-001 in the findings registry.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The enrichment stress may not faithfully model every marker status that the partial index captures.

searchActions.result: Stress prose/helper treat all status != complete rows as the incomplete set expected to drain.
Production includes deferred status, leaves it unrepaired, and bounded backfill selects only pending/partial/failed.
Existing unit test confirms deferred rows are not repaired by normal replay or bounded backfill.

searchLedger.rationale: The stress overclaims full partial-index drain semantics while production intentionally excludes deferred rows from repair.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The index-scan stress may assert lease/cooldown/stale reclaim behavior that diverges from db-state primitives.

searchActions.result: Stress asserts single winner, lease_active backoff, cooldown coalescing, and stale lease reclaim.
Production transaction clears stale leases, returns lease_active/cooldown reasons, and stamps last_index_scan on completion.

searchLedger.rationale: Stress assertions match the production lease/cooldown contract for this focused pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read found no new mismatch in lease acquisition, cooldown, completion, or stale reclaim behavior.

focus: correctness — enrichment-marker-backfill-stress + index-scan-coalescing-stress vitest deep logic

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Assigned slice reviews two non-trivial stress tests against two real state-transition implementations.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt assigned these two durability stress tests and the real repair/lease symbols they exercise.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The stress backlog count could assert rows that real repair never scans.

searchActions.result: The flood test creates pending markers only, then loops on incompleteMarkerCount until real repair drains them.
Real repair scans pending, partial, and failed markers with a bounded limit.
The deferred-marker wording gap is already recorded as R11-P2-001 and was not duplicated.

searchLedger.rationale: No new repair-selection finding: pending-only assertions match real repair, while deferred overstatement is already active as a prior P2.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Duplicate risk covered by R11-P2-001; tested pending path matches source.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The mocked post-insert result could bypass the real repair path and make repaired/scanned assertions meaningless.

searchActions.result: Only runPostInsertEnrichmentIfEnabled is mocked; repairIncompleteMarkers and marker mutators are imported from the real module.
Real repair still marks pending, resolves parsed content, calls post-insert enrichment, records the result, and increments repaired only for complete status.

searchLedger.rationale: The mock controls deterministic completion only; it does not replace the repair selection or accounting logic under review.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct reads confirm real repair path remains exercised.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Concurrent Promise.all acquisitions at the same logical instant may not match the real lease's single-writer behavior.

searchActions.result: The stress expects one acquired result and all other results to return lease_active with bounded waits.
The real lease reservation is a SQLite transaction that sets scan_started_at for the winner and returns lease_active for live leases.

searchLedger.rationale: Stress assertions align with the real transaction-backed single-writer lease contract.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Real source returns the exact structured refusal asserted by the stress.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Cooldown or stale-lease assertions could mismatch completeIndexScanLease and clearStaleScanLease behavior.

searchActions.result: The stress completes the first lease, expects cooldown refusals, then expects one acquisition after cooldown and after stale expiry.
Completion stamps last_index_scan and clears scan_started_at; cooldown returns cooldown; stale leases are deleted before reservation.

searchLedger.rationale: The tested transitions map directly to the real state machine branches.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No branch mismatch found between stress assertions and source.

searchLedger.invariant: The stress tests must use throwaway DB handles and avoid production daemon/socket access.

searchActions.result: The enrichment stress creates an in-memory better-sqlite3 DB per test.
The index stress injects an in-memory DB through db-state.init and does not touch a daemon/socket.

searchLedger.rationale: Isolation statements match the code paths used in this slice.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Both tests use in-memory DB handles and no production runtime socket.

focus: traceability - FINAL completeness sweep: doc/source anchors, shipped behavior documentation, -32001 live and -32002 fail-closed framing

findingDetails.title: Error-code catalog still documents active E429 scan-rate-limit rejections after coalesced success replaced them
findingDetails.claim: The refreshed error-code reference says E429 is the memory_index_scan lease/cooldown rejection and should be retried after the returned wait.
findingDetails.evidence: The active memory_index_scan handler returns createMCPSuccessResponse with success:true, coalesced:true, status:'coalesced', reason, waitSeconds, and nextPollAfterMs for the !lease.acquired branch, while the root README says routine overlapping scans no longer return E429.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the central error code registry; RATE_LIMITED still exists, but that does not prove the current memory_index_scan lease/cooldown path emits it.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The catalog may intend to describe a historical or generic error registry entry, but the wording assigns E429 to current memory_index_scan lease/cooldown behavior and retry guidance.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 only if another in-scope live memory_index_scan branch is shown to emit E429 for lease/cooldown in the current implementation.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search for RATE_LIMITED, coalesced, lease_active, and cooldown found the active handler branch and the root README legacy framing; direct reads did not show an E429 response for this branch.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update the error-code catalog and aggregate catalog wording so E429 remains legacy/registered where appropriate, while routine memory_index_scan lease/cooldown behavior is documented as a coalesced:true success response.

graphEvents.label: E429 doc claim contradicts coalesced scan source

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Assigned review scope spans 73 mixed documentation, spec, changelog, and source files for a final traceability sweep.
